item.title: John Locke and the Ownership of the Self in a Digital Republic
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowPrefaceFew thinkers have shaped the modern political imagination more deeply than John Locke. His arguments about natural rights, government by consent, private property, education, and the limits of political…

item.title: Technical Debt as Cultural Debt
item.description:
Technical Debt as Cultural DebtThere is a button in a hospital that no one is supposed to press.It is not labeled “do not press.” It is labeled something innocuous, something functional, something that once described a process that no longer exists…

item.title: The UI of Authority
item.description:

item.title: NFTs: The Emperor's New Clothes Finally Got Tailored
item.description:
Why the technology that made millionaires out of monkey JPEGs might actually matter now that the casino has closedThe NFT funeral has been premature. While crypto bros wept over their worthless Bored Apes and tech journalists gleefully penned…
